channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: China responds to U.S chip sanctions with a ban on these key materials
item.description: China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony, and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Securing the AI data pipeline with confidential AI
item.description: There is a need to create secure computing capabilities that maintain the privacy and confidentiality of AI models and data sets. The post Securing the AI data pipeline with confidential AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: California teen suspected of being a member of Scattered Spider hacking gang
item.description: US prosecutors charged Remington Ogletree on Oct. 30 with wire fraud in relation to alleged crimes conducted from October 2023 through May 2024.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: US-China Trade Tensions Escalate Over Rare Mineral Exports
item.description: China bans exports of rare minerals to US in response to new semiconductor chip restrictions, escalating trade tensions between nations.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Profiles in Excellence in Government: CIO Achievements 2024
item.description: Join host Luke McCormack as he discusses 2024 federal IT successes with top government and industry technology experts. The post Federal Executive Forum Profiles in Excellence in Government: CIO Achievements 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AT&T Anticipates Earnings Growth with 5G and Fiber Services
item.description: AT&T announces earnings growth expectations over the next three years driven by 5G and fiber services, alongside plans for shareholder returns through dividends and stock buybacks.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Chapman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Agentic AI: Why this emerging technology will revolutionise multiple sectors
item.description: New advancements in GenAI technology are set to create more transformative opportunities for tech-savvy enterprises and organisations. These developments come as data shows that while the GenAI boom is real and optimism is high, not every organisation is generating tangible value so far.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Abanca Leads in AI-Generated Customer Service
item.description: Spanish bank Abanca pioneers AI-generated customer service, aiming for full availability by December.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: China has announced a ban on exports to the United States of gallium, germanium and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 dead-end IT skills — and how to avoid becoming obsolete
item.description: A new area of digital transformation is under way in IT, say IT executives charged with unifying their tech strategy in 2025. Adopting emerging technology to deliver business value is a top priority for CIOs, according to a recent report from Deloitte.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Il Data Act e la data-driven company: quali compiti per il CIO
item.description: Nel corso di quest’anno, i CIO hanno dovuto impiegare parte del loro tempo nello studio del quadro normativo europeo sul digitale, un insieme di leggi che si presentano complementari e, allo stesso tempo, unite dall’intento di favorire l’innovazione delle imprese europee e aprire nuovi mercati.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Biden administration announces these new restrictions on China’s chipmakers
item.description: The U.S. will launch on Monday its third crackdown in three years on China’s semiconductor industry, curbing exports to 140 companies including chip equipment maker Naura Technology Group, among other moves, said two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bipartisan bill would study AI's impact on banking, housing
item.description: A new bill with bipartisan support would commission studies on the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in the banking and housing sectors, where lawmakers have been sounding an alarm about algorithmic price fixing and commercial misconduct enabled by new technologies.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, The Hill

item.title: How AI is accelerating—and devaluing—book publishing
item.description: AI has the power to pump out words in record speed. And already, that’s substantially inflating the book publishing market. Lovers of LLMs are pushing into the traditional publishing system, building their own book-publishing entities.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Panasonic revives its late founder as AI
item.description: Japanese electronics company Panasonic has created an AI version of its founder, Kōnosuke Matsushita, who died in 1989, The Japan Times reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Refactoring AI code: The good, the bad, and the weird
item.description: Generative AI is no longer a novelty in the software development world: it’s being increasingly used as an assistant (and sometimes a free agent) to write code running in real-world production. But every developer knows that writing new code from scratch is only a small part of their daily work.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This whale species is so rare it's never been seen alive. A dissection may decode its mysteries
item.description: Scientists and culture experts in New Zealand have begun the first-ever dissection of a spade-toothed whale, the world's rarest whale species.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bitcoin flirts with $100K as crypto industry rallies for Trump
item.description: Bitcoin has blasted through a series of record highs since President-elect Trump’s election victory as the crypto industry anticipates a significant shift in federal policy.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Artificial intelligence changes across the US
item.description: As more companies implement artificial intelligence for daily tasks, some industry leaders are working to push back against certain aspects of the technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online advertising
item.description: The Competition Bureau of Canada has filed an antitrust lawsuit against Google, accusing the tech giant of abusing its dominant position in the online advertising technology market to stifle competition and harm advertisers, publishers, and consumers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
